subject: Mathematics
title: The Geometry of Origami
description: A quick, practical guide to The Geometry of Origami.

hook.text: Paper folding isn't just art. Mathematicians proved origami can solve problems rulers can't.

buildup.text: With straight folds, you can trisect an angle—something impossible with a compass and ruler.

discovery.text: Origami axioms, formalized in the 1990s, are actually more powerful than Euclidean constructions.

twist.text: NASA uses origami-inspired folds to pack solar panels into rockets, then unfurl them in space.

climax.text: Heart stents, airbags, and satellite arrays all use origami geometry for compact storage.

punchline.text: Paper folding solved problems that rulers couldn't.

quiz.question: What can origami do that classical ruler-and-compass construction cannot?
